title: Tell Atrib (Athribis)
description: Ruins of the ancient capital of the 10th Nome of Lower Egypt. Features remains of temples, domestic quarters, and a Roman bath complex.

title: Tomb of Pnefer
description: Located within Tell Atrib, the limestone tomb of a high official, discovered in 1944.

title: Tell al-Yahudiya (Leontopolis)
description: Site of a massive Hyksos earthen enclosure and the Jewish Temple built by Onias IV.

title: Muhammad Ali Palace
description: A magnificent palace built by Muhammad Ali Pasha, featuring the 'Fountain Kiosk' and extensive gardens blending European and Islamic styles.

title: Fountain Kiosk (Al-Gabalaya)
description: The central feature of the Shubra Palace, characterized by a large water basin, marble island, and colonnades.

title: Delta Barrages (Al-Qanater Al-Khayriya)
description: A historic irrigation structure built by Muhammad Ali Pasha to control the Nile flow into the Delta branches.

title: Damietta Branch Barrage
description: The eastern section of the original Delta Barrages spanning the Damietta branch of the Nile, featuring neo-Gothic turrets.

title: Rosetta Branch Barrage
description: The western section of the original Delta Barrages spanning the Rosetta branch of the Nile.

title: Irrigation Museum (Revolution of the Nile)
description: A museum located in the residency building, showcasing the history of irrigation in Egypt and the construction of the barrages.

title: Child Museum of Qanater
description: Educational museum and science center located within the gardens of the Delta Barrages.

title: King Farouk Rest House
description: A royal rest house used by King Farouk, situated near the barrages providing scenic Nile views.

title: Bridge of Muhammad Ali
description: The iconic pedestrian roadway atop the Delta Barrage structure.

title: Mosque of Al-Zahir Baybars
description: Founded by Sultan Baybars I. While the structure has undergone many renovations, it marks an important Mamluk site.

title: Mosque of Sidi Awwad
description: A historic mosque in Qalyub dedicated to the local Sufi saint Sidi Awwad.

title: Church of the Virgin Mary
description: A major pilgrimage site where the Holy Family is believed to have bathed and washed clothes during their flight to Egypt.

title: The Holy Well (Al-Mahamma)
description: Ancient well located within the Virgin Mary Church complex, believed to have been blessed by the Holy Family.

title: Siryaqus Khanqah (Site of)
description: Site of the grand Khanqah and royal resort established by Sultan al-Nasir Muhammad. It gave the district 'Al-Khanka' its name.

title: Mosque of Sultan al-Ashraf
description: Historical mosque in Al-Khanka, historically associated with the Mamluk sultans who frequented the area.

title: Banha University Museum
description: Holds a collection of artifacts discovered during local excavations in Tell Atrib and the Delta.

title: Banha Old Bridge
description: Historic steel bridge crossing the Damietta branch of the Nile at Banha.

title: Mosque of Sidi Said
description: A revered local mosque contributing to the religious heritage of Qalyub.

title: St. George Church (Mar Girgis)
description: A historic church serving the Coptic Orthodox community in the Qalyub district.
